Previously, GPTQ support was primarily optimized for CUDA backends. Loading MoE architecture

sglang.title: KDA: fuse qkv conv and support stride for fused_sigmoid_gating_delta_rule_update_kernel
sglang.author: strgrb
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

For KDA, qkv conv can be fused to 1 as implemented in GDN.
But fused_sigmoid_gating_delta_rule_update_kernel kernel does not support stride, so some of inputs (q, k, v and b) should invoke extra element-wise kernels.
And GDN can also benefit from this kernel modification.
Modifications

sglang.title: fix(grpc): send last chunk before completion during streaming

Motivation
During gRPC streaming, when the final output arrives, the server was sending only a GenerateComplete response and skipping the GenerateChunk. This means gRPC clients that consume tokens exclusively from chunk messages would miss the last batch of tokens.
The fix ensures the server always

dequantization (awq_dequantize_triton in awq_triton.py) rather than the

coverage, meaning regressions could ship undetected. This PR registers

prepare_for_extend() sets all three Mamba tr

Summary
Qwen3ASRForConditionalGeneration (and its realtime subclass Qwen3ASRRealtimeGeneration) crashes during torch.compile warmup when --enforce-eager is not set. The root cause is a missing dynamic_arg_dims annotation on the @support_torch_compile decorator.
Closes #35868
Root Cause
Qwen3-ASR use

Root cause: CK's MXFP4 MoE GEMM kernels require intermediate_size / tensor_parallel_size

I will try to create a series of PR to address this

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes a bug in the CPU offloading manager where blocks could be accidentally deleted from the cache.
The Bug:
When you call prepare_store([2, 3, 4, 5]) and block 2 is already in the cache:

Block 2 is skipped (already stored)
The manager needs space for blocks 3, 4, and 5
Problem: Block 2 ca

Root cause: scale names were caught by ignore_suffixes before maybe_remap_kv_scale_name could remap them t

Purpose
Add All-to-All (A2A) as an alternative communication backend for Decode Context Parallel (DCP).
The existing DCP implementation uses AllGather + ReduceScatter (AG+RS), which requires 3 NCCL calls per attention layer (AllGather Q, AllGather K metadata, ReduceScatter output). This PR adds an A

vllm.title: [Misc] Add `--attention-backend auto` option

Simple UX change to support "auto" option as alias to not providing the --attention-backend flag at all.
Example:
vllm serve openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo --attention-backend auto

I believe this should bring a bit more consistency across how similar flags are used in vLLM, eg:

--moe-backend
--max-

This PR performs the said split and also adds a few extra generic tests to validate all paths for loading an IO Processor plugin.

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Partial implementation of RFC #34407 Phase 1 (vllm launch vllm online).
This PR adds the FastAPI server for vllm launch, which runs vLLM's OpenAI-compatible API server without GPU inference, enabling tokenization and chat template rendering as a standalone service. gRPC server support (vllm

Optimize Mamba2 SSD prefill Triton kernels through three changes, and clean up dead code.
1. Extended autotune search space
Added new Triton configs to _chunk_scan_fwd_kernel, _chunk_state_fwd_kernel, and _chunk_cumsum_fwd_kernel, covering:

Higher warp counts (num_warps=8) for better memory

Summary
Add B200 nightly correctness test for AsyncTP fusion pass.
Context
The AsyncTP correctness test (correctness_e2e/test_async_tp.py) currently only runs on H100 (optional/nightly). On Blackwell, the FP8 codepath differs: FlashInferFP8ScaledMMLinearKernel (CC >= 100) produces ops that the async

vllm.title: [Docs] Clarify structured outputs configuration for Qwen3 reasoning mode
vllm.author: davzaman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Adding important caveats to the structured output documentation for qwen3 coder models.
Purpose
This PR improves documentation for using structured outputs with Qwen3 Coder models when reasoning is enabled, addressing an undocumented configuration requirement.
Qwen3 Coder output on vllm v0.11.0, v0.

vllm.title: [torch.compile] Stop lazily compiling
vllm.author: zou3519
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #34034
Compile all ranges upfront in VllmBackend
Move Inductor compilation from lazy (first warmup execution) to eager (during VllmBackend.__call__). Previously, PiecewiseBackend compiled each range
on-demand when first encountered at warmup. Now, VllmBackend.__call__ calls PiecewiseBackend.co

vllm.title: [UX] Remove NoOpOffloader log
vllm.author: robertgshaw2-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt: remove Offloader set to NoopOffloader log

vllm.title: [Docs] Document security risks of GPT-OSS Python tool
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add security documentation for the built-in GPT-OSS code interpreter
tool, which executes model-generated code in Docker without network
isolation by default. Update --tool-server help text with a warning.
Signed-off-by: Russell Bryant rbryant@redhat.com

vllm.title: Add PyTorch profiler schedule support with warmup/active iterations
vllm.author: fenypatel99
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Add support for PyTorch profiler schedule-based profiling with warmup and active iterations.
This allows users to configure the profiler to discard initial warmup iterations (which may
contain JIT compilation noise) and only collect data during specific active iterations.
Changes:

Added wa

vllm.title: [CI] Bump `mypy` version to 1.15.0
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Bump mypy from 1.11.1 (Jul 30, 2024) to 1.15.0 (Feb 5, 2025) to get better checking and performance
1.13.0 added a faster-cache optional extra to improve caching performance. We add this in case it helps
We don't bump all the way because newer versions catch things that older versions missed. So we

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix race in non-blocking num_accepted_tokens GPU->CPU copy
vllm.author: tdoublep
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Fix a data race where num_accepted_tokens_cpu (pinned memory) could be read by the CPU before the non-blocking GPU→CPU DMA transfer completes
The bug affects hybrid (Mamba) models with speculative decoding under async scheduling, where no implicit CUDA synchronization occurs between the cop

vllm.title: [RL] [Weight Sync] Guard IPC update-info pickle deserialization behind insecure serialization flag
vllm.author: simon-mo
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Prevent unsafe unpickling of attacker-controlled data in the IPC weight-transfer HTTP path which could lead to remote code execution when ipc transport + dev-mode are enabled.

Description

Added an explicit gate in IPCWeightTransferUpdateInfo.__post_init__ to refuse deserializing ipc_ha

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix passing of activation_type to trtllm fused MoE NVFP4 and FP8
vllm.author: amitz-nv
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix passing activation_type of type int to:

flashinfer.fused_moe.trtllm_fp8_per_tensor_scale_moe
flashinfer.fused_moe.trtllm_fp4_block_scale_moe

Without this fix, the NVFP4 flow doesn't pass the activation, so the default of Swiglu is always used.

vllm.title: Add platform method to enable custom collective ops registration
vllm.author: nkm-meta

Add use_custom_op_collectives() platform method to allow hardware platforms to opt-in to using torch.ops.vllm.* custom ops for collective operations (all_reduce, all_gather, reduce_scatter).
This enables platforms with custom collective backends to register their ops for proper graph tracin

vllm.title: [compile] Fix extra cache save on warm start.
vllm.author: zhxchen17
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Fixing #35708
Currently on the trunk, we will always see an extra cache save even on warm start. This is a bug as warm start shouldn't generate any new file.
To understand what's the root cause, it's important to note that today we have 3 layers of compiler cache on top of each other today:

vllm.title: [Perf] Use dummy M for weight prepacking on x86
vllm.author: tianmu-li
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
On x86 systems without AMX, onednn weight prepacking without known M dimension results in ab layout instead of optimized blocked layouts. The PR uses existing dummy M method for ARM platforms to ensure proper weight prepacking.
Test Plan
Platform: 2S EPYC 9755, DDR5-6400
Server:
export VLLM_
